guardrails: The error-fiber structure is a Lemma C corollary, certified atlas-independently; the parity formula is its regrouping.
Step 9+ makes the selected-xor parity axis first-principles; it does NOT derive the B3 multiplicity matrix (which masks meet which fibers).
No public/PAPP widening or universal-24 claim is made; the B3 15/49 matrix remains a finite certificate (item 7).

metadata.description: Error-fiber parity made first-principles: the fiber structure is a Lemma C corollary (atlas-independent); the selected-xor parity formulas follow from it plus the Direction Lemma.

summary.next_task: Item 4 closed. Proceed to item 5 (the 8 defect-mask B3 classes) of the non-enumerative programme.
summary.reading: The fiber structure (d=1 constant 4-fiber; d=2 three 2-fibers) is a corollary of the Lemma C factorization and holds for every degree-<=2 bijection, atlas-independently. Given it, the selected-xor parity formulas are the regrouping of xor_M E by fiber, and xor_M L = xor_M E is the Direction Lemma. So the selected-xor axis (the B2/B3 cut at delta=4 and the delta=6 three-fiber law) is first-principles, not atlas-fitted.
